channel.title: Scxml on Mohamed Abdelrahman
channel.description: Recent content in Scxml on Mohamed Abdelrahman

item.title: Modeling an Agent Loop as a State Machine
item.description:
Why a State Machine? Link to heading

Most agent loops in the wild are written as ad-hoc while loops with a pile of boolean flags: is_streaming, tool_pending, awaiting_user, cancelled. The flags drift, the edge cases multiply, and “what should happen if a cancel arrives mid-tool-call?” becomes a code-review argument.

Modeling the loop as an explicit finite state machine fixes this. Every state is named, every transition is triggered by a typed event, and there is exactly one place where each rule lives. SCXML — the W3C statechart standard — is a convenient way to write it down because it gives us composite states, hierarchical transitions, and a data model for free.
